claims.claim: We construct a controlled synthetic setting: a frozen single-head attention block is trained via low-rank residuals on $W_k$ and $W_v$ to imitate teachers with known adaptation structure (value-only rank-4, key-only rank-4, and mixed rank-1 key / rank-3 value).

evidence_refs.quote: # Asymmetric Key/Value Adapter Rank Allocation: A Mechanistic Study in Synthetic Frozen Attention

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Across three teacher tasks and seven rank configurations at equal parameter budget, the task-matched asymmetric split consistently and substantially outperforms the symmetric K2/V2 baseline, with mean validation MSE reductions of multiple orders of magnitude.

evidence_refs.quote: # Asymmetric Key/Value Adapter Rank Allocation: A Mechanistic Study in Synthetic Frozen Attention

claims.claim: These results support asymmetric K/V rank allocation as a task-conditioned design choice, not as a universal rule.

claims.claim: Low-rank adaptation (LoRA) freezes pretrained model weights and injects trainable low-rank matrix pairs into targeted linear projections, achieving significant reductions in trainable parameters and optimizer memory with no inference-time latency after adapter merging.
